rulings.Case Title: ANNA FLIDER VS. JOSEPH DUGAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 9, 2011, Line 2. PLAINTIFF ANNA FLIDER MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted in its entirety. Duggan Development, Inc.¿s opposition is stricken because it lacks standing to oppose a motion that is not directed at it. The ruling on this motion does not affect Duggan Development, Inc.¿s rights. Joinder is denied. Allowing the joinder would circumvent the sanction order. rulings.Case Title: KIN FONG VS. LAWRENCE WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 9, 2011, Line 5. DEFENDANT EARL JIANG DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend. The applicable statute of limitations for fraud is 3 years per Code of Civil Procedure 338(d). Plaintiff fails to allege facts showing that the discovery rule or equitable tolling applies to his fraud claim. In order to show that the discovery rule applies, plaintiff must allege the time and manner of discovery and the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence. Furthermore, the fraud claim is not alleged with the sufficient specificity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RENE J. ZUNIGA et al VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 9, 2011, Line 6. DEFENDANT MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS, INC., DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action. The alleged irregularities in the foreclosure sale do not support the relief sought. Nor have plaintiffs alleged tender of the amounts due under the deed of trust. Nor have plaintiffs have alleged sufficient facts to invoke the discovery rule for tolling of the fraud statute of limitations. Plaintiff appears to concede the lack of merit of the fifth and eighth causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
